---
title: "GLAM Parameter Recovery"
author: "Collaborative AI"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{GLAM Parameter Recovery}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5
)

library(fractalGLAM)
library(tidyverse)
library(cmdstanr)
library(bayesplot)
library(posterior)

# Ensure CmdStan is available for the vignette build process
tryCatch({
  cmdstanr::cmdstan_path()
}, error = function(e) {
  cmdstanr::set_cmdstan_path()
})
# Simply attempt to find the path. If it fails, fit_glam will catch it.
# try(cmdstanr::cmdstan_path(), silent = TRUE)

# 1. Simulate Synthetic Data
set.seed(42)
n_subs <- 50
n_trials <- 200

# 1. Create individual differences in Gamma
# We sample a different 'true gamma' for each person
sub_traits <- tibble(
  subject_id = 1:n_subs,
  true_gamma_sub = runif(n_subs, 0.0, 1.0)
)

sim_data <- expand.grid(subject_id = 1:n_subs, trial = 1:n_trials) %>%
  left_join(sub_traits, by = "subject_id") %>%
  mutate(
    value_left = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
    value_right = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
    gaze_left = runif(n(), 0.1, 0.9),
    gaze_right = 1 - gaze_left,
    v_l_scaled = scales::rescale(value_left, to = c(1, 10)),
    v_r_scaled = scales::rescale(value_right, to = c(1, 10))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Calculate the raw GLAM signal
    signal = (v_r_scaled * (gaze_right + true_gamma_sub * gaze_left)) - 
             (v_l_scaled * (gaze_left + true_gamma_sub * gaze_right)),
    
    # 2. Choice probability (Sigmoid is correct here)
    prob_right = plogis(signal * 0.5), 
    choice = rbinom(n(), 1, prob_right),
    
    # 3. RT Drift Magnitude (The Fix)
    # RT should be high when signal is near 0. 
    # We use a 'minimum drift' floor (0.01) to avoid infinite RTs.
    drift_mag = abs(signal) + 0.01,
    
    # 4. Generate RT: Scale it so that small drift_mag = Large RT
    # 1/drift_mag creates the hump at signal = 0
    rt = rlnorm(n(), log(5 / drift_mag), 0.2) 
  )
# set.seed(123)
# n_subs <- 50
# n_trials <- 200
# 
# # True parameters for recovery
# true_gamma_val <- 0.3
# true_v_val <- 1.5
# s_const <- 3e-4
# 
# sim_data <- expand.grid(subject_id = 1:n_subs, trial = 1:n_trials) %>%
#   mutate(
#     # Simulate Z-scored ratings
#     value_left = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
#     value_right = rnorm(n(), 0, 1),
#     # Simulate random gaze distribution
#     gaze_left = runif(n(), 0.1, 0.9),
#     gaze_right = 1 - gaze_left,
#     # Map to 1-10 range as done in prep_glam_data
#     v_l_scaled = scales::rescale(value_left, to = c(1, 10)),
#     v_r_scaled = scales::rescale(value_right, to = c(1, 10))
#   ) 
# sim_data <- sim_data %>%
#   mutate(
#     signal = (v_r_scaled * (gaze_right + true_gamma_val * gaze_left)) - 
#              (v_l_scaled * (gaze_left + true_gamma_val * gaze_right)),
#     
#     # 1. Drift for RT (uses the tiny s_const)
#     drift = true_v_val * s_const * (1 / (1 + exp(-signal))),
#     
#     # 2. Choice Probability (needs a larger scale to be 'behavioral')
#     # We use signal * 0.5 to make choices deterministic enough to show a slope
#     prob_right = plogis(signal * 0.5), 
#     choice = rbinom(n(), 1, prob_right),
#     
#     # 3. RT remains the same
#     rt = rlnorm(n(), log(1 / drift), 0.2)
#   )

# %>%
#   mutate(
#     # Generative GLAM Signal
#     # r = (v_r * (g_r + gamma * g_l)) - (v_l * (g_l + gamma * g_r))
#     signal = (v_r_scaled * (gaze_right + true_gamma_val * gaze_left)) - 
#              (v_l_scaled * (gaze_left + true_gamma_val * gaze_right)),
#     # Drift rate = v * s * sigmoid(signal)
#     drift = true_v_val * s_const * (1 / (1 + exp(-signal))),
#     # Generate Choice (Bernoulli)
#     choice = rbinom(n(), 1, plogis(drift)),
#     # Generate RT (Log-normal to match our likelihood)
#     rt = rlnorm(n(), log(1 / drift), 0.2)
#   )

# Preview simulated data
head(sim_data)

# 2. Fit Model
# Preprocess
processed_data <- prep_glam_data(sim_data)

# Fit using VB
fit <- fit_glam(
  processed_data, 
  method = "meanfield", 
  iter = 20000, 
  s_val = s_const,
# Initialize near the group means to avoid the -inf crash
  init = 0.1#, 
)
# 
# mcmc_fit <- fit_glam(
#   processed_data %>% filter(subject_id == 1), 
#   method = "sample", 
#   iter_warmup = 500, 
#   iter_sampling = 500, 
#   chains = 2
# )

# mcmc_fit <- fit_glam(
#   processed_data, 
#   method = "sample",
#   chains = 2,
#   iter_warmup = 1000,
#   iter_sampling = 1000,
#   # These control the sampler efficiency
#   max_treedepth = 12,    # Increased from default 10
#   adapt_delta = 0.95     # Increased from default 0.80
# )

# 3. Verify Gamma Recovery
# Extract draws
draws <- fit$draws(c("gamma", "v")) %>% as_draws_df()

# Plot Subject-Level Gamma Recovery
plot_gamma_performance(fit, processed_data)

preds <- predict_glam(fit, processed_data, n_sims = 5)

plot_glam_fit(processed_data, preds) +
  labs(title = "Recovered RT Distribution",
       subtitle = "Model reproduces the RT peak at Delta Value = 0")
